Appreciate and again you both exemplify how a business should be run on so many levels.

Thank you again,

Martha A Kehoe

My family has been working with Hunter for years and we are so happy! Hunter is easy to talk to, always responsive, and truly has your best interests in mind. Our investments are in good hands and I never have to worry about things because Hunter has it covered!

Trevor Scotto is a very proficient financial planner and is well versed in all aspects of planning and investing in the various financial markets in our turbulent times. The firm does its best in preserving my investments and in building them for the future.

Retirement planning was a major concern for me until I started working with Trevor and FFG.  His approach is very comprehensive while also covering tax planning and a focus on preserving my hard-earned savings.

Trevor displayed a deep understanding of the conservative financial strategies I preferred. He is always available when I call and he reaches out to me proactively multiple times a year, which I greatly appreciate.

I highly recommend Trevor and his team to anyone seeking financial guidance for their retirement years!

Flavio was very helpful, knowledgeable, and personable. He helped my husband and I plan our future. He helped moved stuff around that we do not understand. He had to make several phone calls and trips to our house. Not everything thing in life is black and white, and he helped fill in the gray area. Now this burden was lifted from me and I have somebody to go too when need be. Thank you for your help. The Pothier's

My husband and I have been working with Hunter for several years and have been very pleased! He is always available and willing to answer all questions with top advice! His expertise allows me to work my profession while he advises investment and retirement planning. I completely trust him and feel very confident with his management and advice! I highly recommend!!

I've worked with FFG since they started. I used to run a large stock portfolio management company that sold software to financial advisors. This means I met a LOT of them.

I stay with FFG because many advisors will tell you how they will outperform the market. These guys know it's REALLY hard to massively outperform the market but VERY easy to underperform the market in down economies. They are focused on right sizing investments to risk appetite, and for me personally I have done pretty well vs the market and a LOT better than the market when it tanks.

These guys aren't $50MM client heavy where you can't ever get time with them, I talk to them several times a quarter and they prove over and over that they have my back as my situation and the economy changes.

Yep I'm writing a review here, but the best review is that I tell everyone I know about how great they are and they have several of my friends as clients :)
Kathy and I had a great Zoom call with Scott on Tuesday. We discussed our financial situation and the 2023 taxes. We feel comfortable with our plan and encouraged with the long term outlook .

One of the best decisions of my life was making that first call to Chris. In a sea of many, Chris stands out as a true gem. His knowledge goes far beyond finance and when navigating unknown water, having Chris at the helm has made all the difference in being able to face the future with confidence. I have had Chris by my side through some challenging years that included a divorce, putting kids through college and planning for my future. i would not feel the security I feel today if it were not for his guidance, counsel and wisdom. Chris understands my financial goals and has helped me achieve them. With his expertise and skill, he has known when to anchor me and when to propel me forward and I have yet to go off course. I am so grateful to have Chris in my life.

As a young business owner, I was advised by my accountant to seek out financial planning services. I interviewed with a number of firms and am so happy that I found FFG and Trevor. FFG has been nothing but attentive, professional, and trustworthy throughout the process. It doesn't hurt that Trevor is also a CPA!  I love that I get highly personalized advice and everyone on the team is exceptionally helpful. As I have worked with Trevor, he has been extremely responsive to any questions I might have and I believe his advice has helped me business grow significantly, plan for retirement, develop good tax strategies, and accomplish personal goals (like purchasing more property!). Thank you FFG!!

I have been dealing with David Rosenstrock at Wharton Wealth for the last seven years as he manages my elderly mother's portfolio. Prior to finding Wharton Wealth, my mother's assets were with a large national firm that churned her account repeatedly and produced lots of bills but not much capital appreciation. Now my mother gets positive returns every year.
Managing a senior's financial wellbeing can be dicey as they age because of risk tolerance, time horizon, and estate planning related issues.  My mother has a trust and has always wanted to leave money for her three children as inheritance.  I found Mr. Rosenstrock to be an excellent listener and very knowledgeable about market history and performance, as  well as  other retirement  topics. On a personal note, what I like best is that he is extremely conscientious about returning calls and emails quickly. For me, that's a big deal and very convenient versus previous experiences I have had with other advisors.

Working in the retirement plan industry has provided me with the opportunity to cross paths with several financial advisors, and I can safely say that Trevor and his team at FFG are by far the best I've ever worked with.  Their availability, intelligence, and willingness to help my wife and I navigate financial decisions, even prior to becoming official clients, is something that is unique to this industry.  In addition, every professional they have recommended, from trust attorneys to life insurance agents, have been equally pleasant to work with.  I have always been one to manage my own finances, but adding the FFG team to help guide us gives me peace of mind that I am maximizing the use of our assets while not being reckless with unsound investments.  I do not easily recommend financial advisors, but I have already provided Trevor's contact information to several close friends who are looking to secure their financial future.

Andy has been working with me and my wife for several years now, and I can't recommend him enough. He has helped us navigate some difficult choices and transitions, like getting married, relocating to another state, and buying our first home. He's helped us organize our finances and start budgeting effectively. He's been kind, patient, and responsive, and it's great to know we share important values. We plan to keep investing with Andy and would recommend him to anyone looking for a financial advisor.

Andy is knowledgable, ethical, and great at communicating. Our portfolio of investments has seen impressive growth since being in his care. Our favorite thing about Andy is how accessible and approachable he is. we know we can ask him anything, he will get back to us, and we always feel better after gaining his perspective. He makes sure to explain things at a level we can understand. We feel very cared for and can't recommend him enough!
Can’t say enough about working with Hazel. She has greatly helped us plan for the future to achieve our retirement goals! We appreciate her sound advice and how responsive she is to our needs.

Trust--We trust Clerestory to manage our assets and to give us sound advice.

Liz and Joe are very knowledgeable and have done an outstanding job in getting us ready for retirement and now being able to enjoy retirement and knowing we will be financially sound. It gives us such peace of mind. Plus they are such nice people!
Such a great experience working with Charles! He gave us great advice and clear understanding of what can be offered for our future. Can’t wait for this financial step with Endeavor.
Extremely knowledgeable and very personable in their approach.  It is refreshing to work with people that care about you personally as well as professionally.

Tim and Dawn have responded to queries within 24 hours which is a better turn around time than we have given them. After moving from Virginia to North Carolina and becoming widowed, I wanted to be in closer touch with my financial advisor than my previous relationship could allow with a 400-mile distance. My Wife and I have been working with Chris for a number of years. He does a great job of explaining things that at first can be very difficult to understand. We are very pleased with our results during the time he has been advising us. I would without a doubt recommend him to friends and family.

advisor_name: Ben Jacobs, MS, CFP®, EA, AIF®
review_text_raw:
We cannot recommend Fully Financial enough! Ben and Cody have been absolutely fantastic in guiding us through our financial journey. Their expertise, professionalism, and genuine care for their clients truly sets them apart.

They took the time to listen to our financial goals and tailored a comprehensive plan that not only met but exceeded our expectations. Their deep understanding of the market and investment strategies gave us the confidence to entrust them with our financial future.

What truly sets Ben and Cody apart is their dedication to their clients' success. They go above and beyond to ensure that we understand every aspect of my financial plan and are always available to answer any questions we may have. Their personalized approach makes us feel like more than just a client; I feel like a valued partner on this journey towards financial security.

Thanks to Fully Financial, we now have peace of mind knowing that our financial future is in capable hands. If you're looking for a financial planner who truly cares about your success, look no further than Ben and Cody at Fully Financial. They are simply the best!

Brett is a honest & truthful advisor who makes you comfortable and shows you what is happening right now with your assets & investments and what can happen with some changes or tweaks to get you into a better environment of your portfolio without pushing you.  Highly Recommended!!!

-- L. Kenna

Having worked with various financial advisors with the AUM model (ranging from robot-advisors to dedicated client managers),  it's refreshing to work with Joe.  We researched a number of professionals and could not be happier with our decision.

In addition to being financially savvy, he's able to make information understandable by adapting to the audience. My husband & I have different levels of financial acumen (& interest). We both walk away from meetings with a clear understanding of where we stand and the next steps we should take.

It's refreshing to work with someone not trying to "dazzle you" with unnecessary information in an attempt to make the process seem like magic.  We appreciate the clear information he provides in a delightful manner that will appeal to both "left brain" and "right brain" people. This isn't a meat-grinder process where everyone gets the same result. Joe learns what the client wants, asks questions to see what the client needs, and is a partner throughout the process.

Bottom line - we feel smart to have selected him, feel confident about our decisions, and look forward to a long-term partnership.

After many years of DIY financial planning, I decided to set a goal to retire early.  I quickly realized that my spreadsheet approach could not handle the interconnected complexities of SS and tax optimizations, Roth conversions, health and long-term care costs, inflation, and other retirement challenges.

This led to my frustrating multi-year search for a qualified and competent fee-only financial planner.  After interviewing several local, national, and institutional financial planners, including signing on and terminating several of those relationships, I finally found Joe.

From the outset, he has exhibited remarkable professionalism and a genuine desire to provide us with a holistic financial plan. He has taken the time to thoroughly understand our financial situation, unique stock programs, goals, and issues and is working towards creating a plan to address all our needs.

As if that was not enough, I genuinely appreciate his ability to involve my wife in the planning process and utilize his exceptional communication skills, breaking down complex concepts into easily understandable terms for both of us. This makes the entire process more accessible, and we feel more confident that we are making informed decisions together.

Thanks to Joe's thoughtful and inclusive approach, my wife and I are working towards a financial plan that aligns with our shared goals. He is ideal if you're looking for a financial planner who values teamwork, communication, and inclusivity. I wholeheartedly recommend his services to anyone seeking personalized and collaborative financial planning experience.

John is incredible to work with. He explains everything thoroughly to make sure we understand, and is always happy to answer our random questions that we send him through email, or jump on a call to have a chat. When we first were onboarding with MDRN Wealth, we met with John every week to get everything squared away and he made us feel very comfortable and confident to work with him - something that's very important when you're trusting someone with your money! We really appreciate everything John has done for us, and we'd highly recommend him to anyone looking for a fee-only financial planner!

I have invested with the Mt Yale Capital Group since their inception and before. Their Team is very knowledgeable and responsive to all of my questions and concerns. I would recommend Mt Yale Capital Group to anyone seeking trustworthy, common sense guidance for their financial well being. I would not still be with them if I was not completely confident and satisfied with their handling of my investments.

Dr. John Henry seamlessly blends his extensive corporate experience and impressive education with a genuine passion for teaching. His ability to convey complex information in a manner that resonates with data scientists, engineers, and actuaries is commendable. For professionals seeking personalized financial solutions, John's transparent fee structure ensures no surprises along the way. A top-notch choice indeed! 💼💡🌟

Peggy and I have been associated with Chad for over 15 years. Throughout our working years Chad and his team helped us invest wisely and lay the groundwork for our impending retirement. We especially appreciated the close interaction we had with Chad and Dave in developing and implementing changes in our portfolio. Chad's recommendations on types of investments we had no prior exposure to was refreshing and something we had never experienced with previous financial planners. Again, we would discuss the pros and cons but ultimately it was our decision to move on them or pass. The timing for us to make the decision to move wasn't the greatest but we never hesitated and have never looked back. Our financial future in retirement is rosy and the relationship we've groomed with Chad and his team is more valuable now than ever.

review_text_raw: I had an excellent experience working with Malissa. Our relationship with FFG began in late 2020 as Shelly approached retirement. We interviewed a number of financial firms—both big and boutique—and quickly realized that we wanted to work with a smaller firm that would be a partner in our retirement journey. We spoke with Trevor at FFG during that interview process. When we asked Trevor why he chose his career path, he shared that his father had suffered financially during the 2008 downturn and he wanted to prevent other retirees from experiencing that stress. That story spoke volumes to us about the type of person that Trevor is and how he would work with us moving forward. We have been with FFG since 2021 and continue to be very pleased with our partnership. We value their responsiveness—they are always available when we have a question or concern. Equally as important to us is the sense of business camaraderie—knowing that Trevor is in our corner and will always offer thoughtful input as we navigate this retirement road.

advisor_name: Ben Jacobs, MS, CFP®, EA, AIF®
review_text_raw: Ben has been our financial advisor for over five years, and our experience has been overwhelmingly positive. The level of care, communication, and service gives us great peace of mind.

My mom has trusted Gary with her financial matters for over 20 years.  I plan to continue to trust him with her accounts, and will soon be adding my own personal accounts to his roster.

review_text_raw: Ben and Cody were referred to us by one of our friends. We spoke to both of them a couple of times before we decided to work with them. As an entrepreneur running a highly successful business, I always felt the weight of financial complexity on my shoulders. That was until I met Doug.

At 53, with a spouse and two children, my main concern was to diversify my wealth, which was predominantly tied up in my company, valued at roughly $50 million. My life objectives included passing maximum wealth to my children and future grandchildren, maintaining a substantial income for personal interests, and focusing on finding a cure for a disease affecting a family member.

Doug's approach to wealth management was transformative. He didn't just manage my assets; he educated me on how capital markets and world economies interrelate. His team completed a financial plan to catalog my current assets and created a strategy to diversify my holdings. To date, I've reallocated $12 million from my company while still retaining full ownership and incurring no debt. His strategy of investing in various market areas, like municipal securities, showed deep market understanding. Doug worked closely with my corporate CFO and estate planning attorney to ensure a comprehensive wealth management strategy, setting up various trusts and partnerships.

The results have been phenomenal. Not only do I have a diversified portfolio, but Doug also set up a 401(k) plan for my company and introduced me to an Investment Banking team for broader advice. His asset allocation alternatives and historic modeling aligned perfectly with my growth and income objectives.

What I appreciate most is Doug's effort to involve my family in this journey. He organized investment seminars for my children and educated my spouse on wealth management strategies.

When the time comes to transition out of the business, I will turn to Doug to help structure the process.


As I look to the future, Doug's role continues to be indispensable. He manages my diversified portfolio, educates my children on money management, and works closely with my team of advisors, ensuring my interests are always prioritized.

In conclusion, Doug is more than a financial advisor; he is a pivotal member of my professional team, guiding me through complex financial markets and ensuring my family's long-term financial security and prosperity.

Hi, My name is Judy Schneider and I live in the Chicago area, and it's my pleasure to recommend my own personal Fee Only Fiduciary Financial Advisor and Certified Comprehensive Financial Planner, Kerry O'Brien.

(I should mention Kerry is also an Enrolled Agent with the IRS and well qualified to file my tax returns each year.)

I hired Kerry in late summer of 2022, and came to be connected with her in a rather unusual way.

It happened on the heels of a bad experience I had with a former financial broker from a well known nationwide financial firm.

That was the first time in my life I had ever used a broker/financial advisor of any kind.  I had recently sold my house in a suburb of Chicago, and wanted advice what to do with the proceeds of the sale because the money was still sitting in a savings account losing to inflation.

This broker had been in the business over 30 years and he was referred me by my dearest friend and her husband.

So I went to this broker/advisor who set up my retirement investments in spring of 2021.

He ended up getting fired beginning of 2022 from his financial firm, losing his license and getting sued.

I felt shaken to the core, details regarding the lawsuit against him are published on the FINRA site, my friends were just as shocked as I was because they trusted him too.

This former broker had taken financial advantage of his elderly client (upon which the lawsuit was based).  Well I'm already in my mid 60s and can't afford have someone try rip me off for my nest egg either.

After that I didn't know where to turn, I lost faith in people who call themselves "financial advisors" as this broker did, and was hesitant to try to find someone else who also calls themselves a financial advisor, but whom I have no reason to trust if I really stopped to think about it.

Before this bad experience I had been under the impression we can trust someone who calls themselves a financial advisor, until I began reading researching and learned the term "financial advisor" is thrown around loosely by people in the financial world.

The more I read, the more I realized how little I know which just furthered my fear because I'm not educated enough in financial matters to pick the right, trustworthy person to help me.

I already work full-time in my own profession as a software engineer and simply don't have time to go back to school to learn all the ins and outs of financial business, just so that I can try prevent someone from taking advantage of me.

I also didn't want to keep my retirement investments at this former financial brokerage firm because by now, I had grown suspicious over the investments he had set up for me.
I knew I had to do something, I had to act fast.

It was then I thought of Terry Savage (of WGN Chicago), because she's a financial advocate, an activist really, a mover and shaker and revered for standing up for people especially the little guy.
I knew I could trust Terry, so I tried reaching out to her (through her AskTerry site where folks can post questions.)

To my surprise and relief she answered me, and after briefly explaining my situation she did a whole lot more than just answer my question in email.

We got on the phone, and after showing Terry the investments this former broker set up for me, she immediately pointed out they were completely inappropriate for somebody my age in their 60s!

(So my gut was right, that broker put my nest egg at risk for someone my age, but I'll bet he made a nice commission. I felt angry.)

I knew I could trust whomever Terry told me I can trust, and she put me in touch with Wealthramp.org, (an organization by Pam Krueger, close associate of Terry).

I was connected to Kerry O'Brien through
Wealthramp.org, and after interviewing Kerry and hiring her, the rest is history.

Kerry has been my Fee Only Fiduciary Financial Advisor and Certified Comprehensive Financial Planner ever since.  It was the smartest decision I made, has brought me relief and peace of mind.

I learned Pam Krueger personally vets each Fee Only Fiduciary Financial Advisor and Certified Comprehensive Financial Planner before bringing them onboard onto Wealthramp.org.

This is a great organization because there is no way I would know how to interview such a person nor what questions to ask since I'm not educated in financial business myself. Someone could tell me anything and I could be lead to believe them.

So basically, Wealthramp.org is a means by which the public can find a vetted, highly educated, qualified and experienced Fee Only Fiduciary Financial Advisor and Certified Comprehensive Financial Planner.

I've gotten quite an education from Terry and Wealthramp.org, and this is why I've chosen Kerry O'Brien as my personal Fee Only Fiduciary Advisor and Certified Comprehensive Financial Planner, here is what I learned:

#1.
A Financial Broker is not a Financial Advisor.
Brokers work for a broker dealer.
(I didn't know the difference before this.)

#2.
Even if someone claims they are a Financial Advisor but they are employed by a brokerage firm, then that is still a conflict of interest because they are being paid by the brokerage firm or being paid in commissions for steering their client's money into incentivized investments.

So this type of Financial Advisor's first allegiance is going to be, has to be, to their employer/brokerage firm, or to the investment company, versus what it should be which is to what is in the best interest of their client.

I learned there is only one category of Financial Advisor which is truly trustworthy, and must always put what is in the best interest of their client first, and that is a Fee Only Fiduciary Financial Advisor.
(Terry Savage constantly harps on this.)

People should know no one works for free, for my own peace of mind (especially after the bad experience), I'd rather pay my Fee Only Fiduciary Financial Advisor directly and know I'm not being sold something or lied to. Fee only Fiduciary Financial Advisors are held to the highest most stringent standards in the U.S.

#3.
Although some folks may feel comfortable enough venturing out on their own with no Financial Advisor at all to represent or guide them, by going to Vanguard or Schwab or TD Ameritrade or T. Rowe Price to set up their own investment accounts, this wasn't for me.

I would probably choose the wrong investments and lose part or all of my nest egg, or keep it all in cash to be safe, thus losing to inflation.

#4.
I knew I needed help investing my retirement nest egg appropriately for someone my age, and managing my cash flow to put the money in the right place for the right flow, with some handholding and with attention to taxes and Medicare.

In other words, I needed a Full Service Certified Comprehensive Financial Planner.

I would not find that level of help by venturing out on my own with Vanguard or Schwab or TD Ameritrade or T. Rowe Price.

#5.
I learned the difference between Fee Only and Fee Based advisors.

Terry Savage always advises her readers to seek a Fee Only advisor.

Since Fee Based advisors are still motivated to earn commissions by selling their clients certain products, once again this presents a conflict of interest, exactly what I was trying get away from because this by definition can not be in the best interest of the client.

The most straightforward, honest business arrangement one can have with their Financial Advisor, is that of a Fee Only Fiduciary Financial Advisor, who are bound by the most stringent standards and require a binding legal and ethical fiduciary agreement between advisor and client.

"Financial advisors" who are not true fiduciaries, use what's called the "Suitability Standard".

That is to say they're obliged to sell their clients something which is suitable, but that is not the same thing as what is in the best interest of their client. There's quite a difference.

Some financial advisors will not come out and admit that they are not operating under the fiduciary duty to their client.  But there is a way to flush them out.

Terry Savage advises folks out here who believe their in financial advisor is a true fiduciary, to ask that advisor if they would be willing to sign a piece of paper which states they are operating under the fiduciary duty to their client, and to state they are not earning commissions are getting compensated in another way.
Almost guaranteed that individual will not sign thst piece of paper.
What should that tell us?

Why I chose Kerry:

Kerry is a highly skilled, educated and experienced Fee Only Fiduciary Advisor and Certified Comprehensive Financial Planner.

It's also a comfort to me she is younger because I expect she will outlive me, so that I won't be put in the position of being elderly and losing her only to have to find someone new to start building trust again.

But it's not enough to be highly skilled, educated and experienced, because Kerry also possesses excellent communication skills and the sensitivities required to be successful in this kind of work, she is a very good listener and passionate about what she does.

In the beginning, Kerry had me fill out an in-depth questionnaire to learn about my risk tolerance, so that she could best advise me.

Then over a period of time, as I grew to trust Kerry more she was able to make further financial suggestions that she felt would be in my best interest, and so later after getting over my bad experience I was able to listen to Kerry and take her good advice when I was ready.

As I look at where I'm at today compared to the financial broker debacle first half of 2022, I'm in a much better place now.

Not just financially but also mentally as it's all connected, my mental state has improved, stress levels have dropped, I can sleep better at night, I don't think about money all the time, or worry with that sinking feeling in my stomach about how much money a non-fiduciary financial advisor/broker is ripping me off for, what they're hiding from me.  I totally prefer things be out in the open.

Trust is everything when it comes to your money, it means your survival in old age especially for unmarried women with no spouse and second income.

I half jokingly told Kerry that what I worried about most as I age, is basically Maslow's hierarchy of needs - having enough good quality food to eat and living in a safe neighborhood.

But I wasn't really joking and Kerry understood it was a real concern. She assured me that she will do everything she can to make sure to address those concerns. I believe her, I know she will make her best faith effort. That's everything. There's not much more I could ask.

There is no such thing as a best faith effort with a non-fiduciary financial advisor or a broker. Unless perhaps you're their mother.

I wholeheartedly endorse Kerry O'Brien.

Based on my bad experience, I think many if not most Americans are still in the dark about the pitfalls and ripoffs of brokers and non-fiduciary financial advisors.

I've also read that unfortunately there's not a lot of government oversight so consumers are at risk if they don't know what to look for, and this is something Terry Savage and Pam Krueger have been trying to change starting with educating the public.

I feel if more people knew about the inherent consumer risk in the world of brokers/financial advisors, they would want to drop them and migrate over to exclusively relying on Fee Only Fiduciary Financial Advisors.

Thank you for taking the time to read this, I hope this helps the readers out there.

(I might also recommend if you haven't yet, reading Terry Savage's online articles were folks from all over write her with their financial questions.)

I met with Gerry and he reviewed my holding and prepared a comprehensive ‘Mutual Fund Fee Analysis’ which contrasted my present holdings fee structure with equivalent low cost Index Funds.  The results were startling and the projected savings going forward were more than enough to convince me to go with him and his investment philosophy.  I had a 9 year history with my old adviser, so dissolving that relationship was very difficult for me, but it had to be done.  Besides being a very talented and multifaceted adviser, Gerry is a very nice guy and listens to your suggestions and concerns and then offers the advice you’re paying him for.  I recommend him to everyone who asks me ‘who is your guy?’

Gerry's staff including Patti are equally top notch.  Great results and great comfort all the way.

If you want help with big picture financial planning and want an advisor who focuses on the the long-term management of your money who then can execute the plan for you day in and day out, Gerry and UFP are worth a look. I found Gerry by researching investment professionals who are part of the Garrett Planning Network, which is comprised of advisors who act as fiduciaries, putting their clients needs first.  You'll find that Gerry doesn't earn any commissions for buying or selling anything for your portfolio, so there's no conflict of interest between your needs and his. Instead he charges what I believe to be a reasonable fee for his services, which for us includes portfolio management, tax planning and income tax services.  His fee was in-line or lower than the majority of advisors I researched. His investment philosophy isn't particularly sexy, focusing on diversification across asset classes, long term investing and avoiding individual stocks. So if you're looking to invest in the latest fad and make a killing overnight, he's not the guy for you.  His approach is a good match for me and my family and our needs, providing us with the tools to monitor our investments as well as play around with models for seeing how our discretionary and non-discretionary spending impacts our investments over time. Prior to using Gerry, I was doing a decent job investing myself (given that I've worked in the financial sector for years), but handing "the keys" over to him has eliminated all the second guessing and prevented me for making decisions that are impacted by my emotions; I no longer wake up in the middle of the night worrying about my investment decisions. Oh, and he's always quick to respond when I send him I have a question or need guidance on anything that could impact my financial situation.

Joe is exactly what I need in a financial advisor.  He charges a flat fee, so he focuses on listening to me.  He's knowledgeable, patient, and explains the reasoning for each step.  I fully endorse him!

As I write this review my BMM personal financial advisor is Nate Tomkiewicz and in all truth of the five financial advisors I have had over the course of my investment career he outperforms the other four combined.

My journey with BMM began when I participated in one of their promotional events at which I was given an opportunity to meet with several of their advisors to ask questions and learn more about BMM investment strategy.  It was a good session that ended with an offer by BMM to review my present portfolio and meet again to discuss potential improvements they had found, if any. This overview was offered purely as an academic endeavor. No fee or further obligation implied or stated.  Well this was the first of many amazing moments with BMM.  I am an engineer by trade and I live by empirical data.  Here was a financial advisor saying, lets stop talking and let BMM do some work to demonstrate proficiency and performance. They reviewed my portfolio and true to their word they presented a list of actions with quite a few recommendations that I was free to pursue with my present financial advisor who had missed them.  At this point I was a little stunned by these dealings with BMM.  Here was the first financial advisor I had ever met who said what they did and did what they said. I made a decision right right then and there, moving forward BMM was going to manage my retirement savings and my partnership with Nate was born.

From this point on Nate and I met for frequent portfolio reviews.  I never have to chase Nate down to get some of his time and attention, he chases me down.  He engaged all aspects of every financial move we made with much attention to detail. By the time each of our sessions was at an end I had a clear list of action items he was going to pursue as well as what other BMM personnel would be doing.  Together we worked on rolling over most of my 401K and consolidating into IRAs before I had even retired.  It is a common occurrence for Nate to suggest making a move in such a way as to be most tax advantageous that never would have occurred to me.  He is knowledgeable and has cutting-edge information at his fingertips.  Recently I retired and just when I thought the surprises were over Nate sits me down and starts to discuss this new phase of my life and how hobbies and activities can enrich my life enjoyment.  He just took off his advisor hat and seamlessly morphs into life coach.  Another amazing BMM moment that had never happened with previous advisors.

I have to take this opportunity to mention Allen Harris and the BMM professional staff.  We are living in some turbulent times and it is wonderful that BMMs goal is not only to create wealth but to preserve it.  In his chats Allen always strives to calm the waters and take the long view.  I appreciate his stability.  Kudos to the folks behind the scenes like Zack, Lauren, Tammy, and others who are working to make me successful.

I wish all of you reading this review to have a long happy, healthy, and wealthy life, you have earned it!  And you could do no better than to let Nate and the BMM crew get you there!

You learn very quickly during this process that a Will is simply a wish list, but the Trust is the playbook on the execution of the Will and so many more details beyond finances.  If you haven't done a trust yet, and plan to pay on your own, these are typically $2K+.

I also use their inhouse financial planners to take a snapshot of my total finances versus my long term needs and goals.  This is an in-depth look under the covers, with a corrective action, if needed.  As my income changes, my investments fluctuate, and my goals adjust, I can reach back to the financial planning team to recreate a current plan for me.
If you are happy with your money management company, make sure they are doing more than picking stocks and funds for you.  Make them work harder for you and your family, as BMM does for mine.

Then a significant life transition occurred 10+ years ago. The company for which I had worked for several years merged with a larger firm which resulted in cash payment for our company stock as it was an employee owned firm. So, with a sizeable check in my hand a decision had to be made as to what to do with it. Obviously I was not alone in this, so we decided to invite Jeff Hall of Rather & Kittrell to speak to our group and respond to questions. I was very impressed with Jeff's presentation, and after further private discussions, my wife and I decided to place our future financial wellbeing to Jeff and R&K.

Much of our time together in the early days was spent providing Jeff with our life circumstances, hopes and dreams, family relationships, risk tolerance, etc., and through that confirming our trust in Jeff and his judgement. He encouraged us to become debt free (which we have), and other matters which were beyond pure investing but very important  to our long term wellbeing.

Then came a second transition. We both retired and thus entered a new phase of life with SS instead of bi-weekly paychecks. We turned over our 401K/IRA's to Jeff to invest consistent with our previously agreed strategy. All has gone well; in particular his calming influence to stick with "the plan" even in difficult market circumstances.

A final personal word... Jeff has become a good friend, much like a member of our family. He is a consummate professional backed by a great organization; and very important to us, also a strong Christian business and family man.

I have worked with Chris Snyder, handling my investments for over 30 years.  During this time I developed a personal relationship with Chris and am very pleased with the personal attention he has paid to my investments.
    When I was getting ready to retire I looked into what my retirement income would be.  I wanted to maintain my current lifestyle and hoped to maintain my existing income.  I did some number crunching and knew I would fall a little short.  I had $23,000 that I could invest in hopes that it would eventually provide a supplement to my monthly income.
    I started an interview process with several investment companies.  I provided them with the financial figures I had been analyzing, knowing I would fall a little short.  They all said that if i invested with them that I would meet my goal, except Chris.  He said I would fall a little short.  Truth will out, I invested with Chris because I felt he was the only one that was honest with me.  The first year was a terrible year for the economy. Not only did my investment not grow but I lost $3,000.  Chris recommended I ride it out and not make any changes. The same advice he has given over the years.  My investment start to grow, sometimes rapidly other times slow but always growing.  For several years now both my wife and I take a monthly supplement that comes from the annual growth of the investment and allows the principal to continue to grow.
    Pillar Financial has simplified my tax returns by sending a copy of all documents pertaining to my investments directly to my tax preparer.  Also, Chris is in direct contact with my tax preparer and there have been several incidents where they have communicated directly with each other.
    There was one incident of personal attention that bears mentioning.  My family have all moved to Arizona.  On a visit to them in April of 2019 we were looking around the countryside with the thought that we might want to move there also. As luck would have it, we looked at a house that my wife fell in love with.  We spent that afternoon in the realtor's office with the realtor on one phone negotiating with the seller and me on another phone with Chris who was advising me on my negotiating position.  He finally came up with a figure that I could take out enough to make a cash offer without incurring a tax liability.  It took three phone calls while Chris did the number crunching with my investment but the cash offer was accepted.  We are planning to move into the house as soon as the remodel is completed.  Even though we will be spending the rest of our days living in Arizona, we plan to keep our investment portfolio with Pillar Financial.
    Over the years I have recommended Pillar Financial to family members and friends.  I was, and still am confident that they would get the same professional and personal treatment that I have received.  I highly recommend Pillar Financial for anyone who is looking for an investment counselor.

advisor_name: Michael R. Acosta, CFP®, ChFC®, CSLP®
review_text_raw:

- Connections to other resources

Mary Sanchez

Doug was very helpful in my transition  from 30 plus years with Morgan Stanley to his firm. So far I've  been pleased  with his  investments.

I chalk it up to the Cosmos.

I don’t know what brings us in tune to each others rhythms and/or function. Our personas unfold naturally with no edge, no ego and an unfiltered rawness you try so hard to find these days out in the world.

Over the course of the last few years we haven’t been able to see each other as much to say the least. It's weighed heavy on me not having him down the street where we could meet up for coffee and just shoot the shit with each other about anything that comes to mind. Those were the times.

We found our wives, moved to different cities, had kids. You know this story. Through a lot of the challenges we’ve managed to devote time aside to catch up with one another whether it be through pictures, quick chats or FaceTime, etc. Sprinkled in on just checking in on each others lives, Eric has not only guided me financially but he has also guided me through a lot of life's idiosyncrasies. At the time , these things seem monumental. We never know what can happen day to day. I'm lucky to have him at a moments notice.

Eric’s what they call a Stalwart. Reliable - Hardworking - Loyal. Effortless could also be used to describe his devotion.  Much of this has to do with Erics persona and his demeanor. His essence is pure and his candor is that of pianist - Hitting all the right notes - on time, in tone and precise. At the end of the day, aren't we all looking for that on the other side of the coin no matter what the basis of our inquiries are, or the tutelage that we seek?

I think so.

Whether it be financial advice, legalities or better still - anything under the sun that requires unconditional care - You want my guy in your chicken coop.

We discussed the usual topics: investing, asset allocation, various retirement accounts, etc. However, we also discussed holistic life planning items such as: life insurance, work-life balance, estate planning (healthcare proxy, power of attorney, wills), and even topics as open-ended as where to live and raise a family (we're both around the same age with young families). These topics are incredibly complex, but Eric had a way to synthesize them and distill them down to be understandable, manageable, and actionable, which I really appreciated (and needed).

As to be expected, Eric quickly advanced at Personal Capital and the firm matched me up with another advisor, a bit to my dismay. I emailed Personal Capital to let them know that I was very happy with Eric and I wished him the best. Much to my delight, Personal Capital reached out to Eric and he agreed to continue to have me as a client even though he was starting a new position with increased responsibility and a whole host of new clients - truly a gem!

I was bummed to find out Eric was leaving Personal Capital because I received so much value from him and really enjoyed connecting with him and getting to know him as a person. However, I was simultaneously excited and happy for him and his family to take the next steps in their journey.

TL;DR

Eric is a rare mix of person with all the qualities a great coach possesses. I highly recommend him!

I connected with Eric when we were both young finance professionals in San Diego. Eric helped me get set up with my first personal investment account, and was always a trusted resource with any questions I had. Eric has referred me several mortgage clients over the years, including himself.

I was fortunate to get to work with Eric during his time at Personal Capital where he managed multiple accounts for our family.  I appreciated his experience in the space and how he willingly shared that knowledge.  His willingness to ask penetrating questions, an innate curiosity and insightful perspectives differentiated him from others in the field.   It’s always a pleasure to engage with someone who has a similar mindset.  Eric’s holistic perspective – including finance, health, longevity will serve him well as a life planner.  With an open mind and a sincere interest in his clients, along with a broader view of where they want to go in life, allow him to provide a unique and valuable service that will have a meaningful impact on his clients and himself.

David and Jack are a great team to help you accomplish your financial goals. I have worked with them for many years. They always have positive, thoughtful, and supportive suggestions to guide you, and help you look at things in different ways. Very quick to respond.

Amir is a fiduciary, which means he is legally obligated to act in my best interests. Amir is incredibly knowledgeable about financial planning and investing. He is also very patient and understanding. He took the time to get to know my financial situation and my goals, and he developed a plan that was tailored to my specific needs.

I graduated in 1985 and lost touch with him until Facebook came along, and we reconnected immediately. My Intuition was exactly correct. He was one of the youngest vice presidents at Morgan Stanley Smith Barney working in Beverly Hills. I became a urologist, and was only a little bit into my practice when we reconnected so I didn't have enough money to invest with Jorey, but he saw my potential and took our friendship into account and brought me out to Beverly Hills for a financial meeting.

It was the best decision I ever made. He has successfully managed my money for over 20 years. He is reachable by text within five minutes, which is unusual, because I'm on the East Coast and he's on the West Coast. I think the man literally never sleeps.

One of the things that makes him very unique is that I've had wealth managers before. They don't want my input or my opinion. They have a formula that works for them and it is what it is. Jorey not only had a unique ability to explain all his thinking, but literally entertained any business or stock idea that I had with his own research to either tell me that it was worth a shot, or to explain why it was not. Other than having an extremely valuable, and very close friend, I can safely say that the reason that I am financially secure today with my minimum business knowledge is Jorey Bernstein. I give him my highest recommendation.

April 11, 2023
I recently contacted Clint Haynes, a certified financial planner and founder of NextGen Wealth in Lee’s Summit, Mo. I ran across NextGen Wealth while doing a search for an important income tax-related subject that has been vexing me and was not explained in ordinary language anywhere online until I found a blog post written by Mr. Haynes on the nextgenwealth.com website. The subject concerned IRS taxes owed on ordinary income as opposed to taxes owed on long term capital gains and qualified dividends. These are taxed differently but exactly how is what I wanted to know.
In his post, Mr. Haynes clarified the issue and gave examples that answered my questions.
I followed up with a phone call to NextGen Wealth hoping I would be able to speak with Mr. Haynes directly as I wanted to confirm my understanding of what he had written and because I had some other questions about retirement. My call was answered by a very friendly and professional person who put me through to Mr. Haynes. We had what was for me a quite illuminating ten or fifteen minute conversation on both the original subject of my call and some additional questions I had about retirement and wealth management which, as a certified financial planner, is in his wheelhouse. . Mr. Haynes emphasized that he was not an accountant and was not qualified to give tax advice. But given the several hours I spent online including with the IRS and major accounting firms, trying and failing  to get some basic information about tax ramifications of different categories of income, and then realizing how concisely Mr. Haynes wrote on the subject that concerned me, I concluded that he was brilliant and trustworthy. As such I  would certainly recommend Mr. Haynes  to anyone who needs the services of a financial planner.

I was referred to Royce and his team by a friend who is in the field of investments.  He indicated that Royce is one of the best in the business. Royce and his team are always available for any questions I may have and always take the time to make sure that my needs are being met.  in addition, they provide monthly summary of market conditions and host client webcasts around current economic factors.
Charles Luong was very helpful with my questions I had regarding finances. He explained and was very detailed. I will definitely contact him in the future.

I consider myself very financially literate, but I was getting overwhelmed by the plethora of information out there on how to successfully plan for financial goals and retirement. Stephan took all the guesswork away, created a clear path, and provided much needed reassurance. We look forward to our meetings and are excited to share our progress each time! We always come away with concrete action items and it feels good to take ownership of our plan with his guidance. Thanks so much, Stephan!!

I have been a client of Russ for several years and am very satisfied with his advice and support. Russ in an independent, fee-based adviser. He focuses on conservative and low cost investment options. He worked with me to design a comprehensive financial plan with an investment portfolio constructed based upon available financial assets, investment goals and investment risk tolerance. I take comfort from the fact that as an independent adviser, Russ does not build portfolio's with his own firm's proprietary investment products. His investment style is long-term and avoids fads.

Russ also provides me with advice and assistance on other important matters, including life-style and “next chapter” planning. He likewise works with third party experts on matters such as planning for Medicare. Russ has got strong communication skills and is always responsive when I have a question and also is pro active on reaching out to me as a client.

Intentional Living Financial Planning has been a breath of fresh air for our family.  Their thoughtful, personalized service has better equipped and empowered us to be an active partner in our investment journey and not feel like just another account number, which we have experienced with past advisors.  We would strongly recommend ILFP to anyone seeking a trusted guide and knowledgeable resource for holistic, caring, and tailored financial advice.

advisor_name: Richard J. Archer, CDAA, CFA, CFP®, MBA
review_text_raw: My wife and I have been with the Archer team for almost 3 years. Throughout, they have been responsive, thorough, and professional. All of the staff are excellent communicators, in person or via email/texts! The entire experience has brought us a great sense of security - from the initial “get to know you” conversations, with Rich doing an excellent job of listening, to the mid-course corrections when life changes. Initially they brought great organization to our portfolio, and our retirement is organized and easy to track. Whenever it’s time for us to do some homework, they are great at helping us with our end of the tasks.

I have been with Cardinal Wealth and Anthony for over a year.  Anthony was the one to give me confidence that I could retire.  He is always an email or a phone call away, if I have any questions.  I have been enjoying my retirement, even in these tough economic times.  Anthony always has good reason for not worrying about my finances.  I am looking forward to a long relationship with Cardinal Wealth.
I have only been a client for a few months, but so far I appreciate the transparency and availability of John/MDRN Wealth and look forward to working with him more.

I've known Mike since we served together at Vandenburg AFB in 2018-2019.  I was his Individual Mobilization Augmentee while he was the Mission Support Group Commander.  I observed first hand his leadership and dedication to the mission.  As a client of Next Mission Financial, I've seen Mike's passion and commitment to helping people feel more confident with their finances.  My wife and I trust him with our financial planning.  His integrity and his commitment to service and excellence in above reproach.  We are very happy and fortunate to be working with him.

My fiance and I have been working with John for a few months now, and he's played a huge role in helping us prepare for the future. From helping us with our retirement and brokerage accounts, to guiding us through our options and setting a strategy for how we will approach finances after our wedding. The customer service is great, and John always makes himself available anytime we have questions.

I was looking for an advisor that would give solid advice as my wife and I approach retirement, without conflicts of interest.  Stephan has lived up to my high expectations.  Concrete advice that is easy to understand, flexibility, availability, and no conflicts of interest.  Meets our needs completely.

Having been with a previous planner a long time, I thought I knew what I wanted. I wanted a mix of coach/cheerleader that let me lead, but who brings expertise and knowledge I lack. Stephan has been that--and MORE! He's incredibly smart, great human skills, and a subtle and kind desire to help that's very comforting. This mix of technical savvy and support is an awesome fit for my needs. I like being coached and supported by Stephan and think we'll be together long term! I certainly hope so.

My husband and I highly recommend Russ for your financial planning needs. We are long time clients and have trusted his steady guidance and timely advice. He helped us set goals which enabled us to strike a great work/life balance and now in retirement, we are living the plan/dream!

advisor_name: Michael R. Acosta, CFP®, ChFC®, CSLP®
review_text_raw: I have had the pleasure of working  with Michael Acosta to accommodate mutual clients and their financial needs for many years and he has been fantastic! I had the opportunity to experience his process first hand before I personally became a client and I was blown away by his knowledge, consistency and his genuine passion for wealth management. He is a joy to work with and has helped my family and I build a solid, financial foundation that will allow us to successfully retire when we are ready for it. I am confident in his recommendations and I follow his advice wholeheartedly. He is the best of the best!

You’re not just getting a financial planner with joining Riki’s team. You’re getting a piece of mind. It’s such a huge relief knowing that there’s someone so qualified out there to reach out to at any time. He puts your BEST interests first and makes you a priority. From his personalized video messages every quarter on things relevant to you; to his ability to break things down to a level where a simple minds (such as mine) can understand. I’ve never been so confident and assured about finances and my own personal money goals as I have been with Riki by my side. One of the first meetings I had with him he made the comment that “anything with a dollar sign in front of it I can help you out with.” And I’ve learned over time that this is the most accurate depiction of his services. My only regret is not starting sooner! Riki is the man!!!!! If I could give more than 5 stars I would.

Having Doug in my corner managing my investment needs has been tremendously helpful. Aside from his deep knowledge as a Certified Financial Planner, he possesses great communication skills as well. Additionally... whenever I have a question or need guidance with my investment strategy, Doug has always been extremely responsive and accessible.

Bottom line... Doug is the best!

I was referred to Michael Raimondi by dear friends I trust, and they did me the biggest solid. Michael is that rare gem of  a sharp money guy and good-hearted human. I was nervous about being judged for my current state of finances when I began working with Michael, but he was so supportive and highlighted what I was already doing well and has gently yet quickly guided me to more financial stability, less financial strain, and debt reduction without severe constraints. Michael has made what felt super complicated and overwhelming for me initially simple and understandable. Now when I pay bills I feel a sense of excitement rather then dread, knowing I'm working towards achieving my financial goals that Michael has helped me prioritize. Whenever I reach out to Michael to ask for financial guidance, he is extremely responsive and I know he's looking out for me. Working with Michael has been the best investment I've ever made and it's paying dividends, literally!

After a rather exhaustive search, and experience with several other Financial Advisors, I was thrilled to meet and start working with Doug. Financial Planning is both an art and science, something that requires time and movement from "the weeds"(across many subjects) to the conceptual and psychological realities of the investor. Doug gets it! I feel like I'm in very good hands and have a long time partner(and friend) as I prepare to enter retirement and start living my best life.

I am single, on the cusp of retirement, and needed a financial advisor relating and understanding.  I found Doug who has been precisely what I was looking for and needed.  His business acumen is only over-shadowed by his one-on-one personal touch.  I could not recommend him any more highly.

My husband and I have been using Anthony for the last year.  He is always willing to help and provide guidance and charts to make us feel at ease and understand the situation.  Retirement is stressful in itself, and with his guidance he was able to show us the way and feel comfortable.  He is very personable and maintains communication in situations that arise.  We appreciate his knowledge and information, and would strongly recommend him to anyone.

advisor_name: Richard J. Archer, CDAA, CFA, CFP®, MBA
review_text_raw: We have been with Richard for about 2 years.  His team is thorough, responsive, and anticipatory.  I appreciate annual reviews and meeting throughout the year.  I look forward to our ongoing relationship.  I recommend them without hesitation.  We transferred our retirement accounts and underwent a comprehensive assessment of our holdings and investor risk.  They have excellent follow through, a reminder system for you to accomplish your assignments.  I feel confident that our financial decisions will be enduring.

Doug at Life Point Planning takes time to learn where you stand with your current investment/retirement strategy.  He will use that information to offer up a revamped strategy that will maximize your portfolio while keeping your risk tollerence at a level you are comfortable with.  He is a constant student of his profession searching for investment and tax saving scenarios that will benefit his customers.  He is unlike the majority of this profession and only feels good with his win when he knows he created a win for his customer.  He is in this for the long haul.

I have only been with NexGen Wealth for relatively short period of time but my portfolio is looking much better than it did with my former financial advisor. That makes me a very happy camper. Thanks for all you do, Clint. I really appreciate it!

I'm eager to write this review for Doug. While I know that words don't teach, I hope that my effort here will inspire you connect with him personally and discover the benefits of collaboration with him, for yourself.  Doug brings a broader perspective to financial advising.  His approach creates ease, financial clarity, and straight forward communication.  Dougs helps clients to focus on the things that are truly essential in the creation of the abundance we are all seeking in life.  Last year, I went through a painful divorce and mid-life transformation.  Doug was a calm, clear signal in the storm, you might say, showing up for me again and again with wisdom and compassion.  Without reservation, I can say that he is lined up in his integrity and purpose.  I encourage you to give him a call, a joyful life awaits.

I was introduced to Mike by a mutual friend and have been blessed to have him as my financial planner for over a year now. Mike has provided wise counsel, education, and direction in helping me better invest and manage my savings and grow my retirement. I appreciate the knowledge shared and the MANY questions he's answered for me as we build our relationship. I look forward to an amazingly fruitful long-term relationship!

Having a financial advisor I trust and know cares about me and in turn, my money is not something I thought was possible. I just followed what other people had done with these big companies. It all felt very transactional and impersonal. When I started working with Ian, that completely changed. He is an honest, caring advisor that goes above and beyond. Ian is someone I trust and we have a collaborative relationship even though he manages my portfolio. As a therapist, this is important to me. He is brilliant in the ways I am not while still educating me! Just a stellar advisor.
Confident in the direction he advised my wife and I to help our parents in their retirement years. He was also extremely good at walking us through things in an easy to understand way.

MDRN Wealth has been absolutely amazing! I have had all of my questions answered and feel confident in how my money is being taken care of. They have put my mind at ease and I am excited to continue working with them as I grow my wealth.
Attentive, active listener, polite. Charles was awesome to meet with!

advisor_name: Richard J. Archer, CDAA, CFA, CFP®, MBA
review_text_raw: I've known Richard Archer for over 20 years and he "walks the talk".  He is trustworthy, honest, and genuinely cares for the people he serves. Richard understands our goals and uses his gifts and knowledge to make sure we are on the right tract for reaching them.  We look forward to our annual review with Richard and his team.  They are proactive in making sure they have answers even before we ask the questions!

advisor_name: Richard J. Archer, CDAA, CFA, CFP®, MBA
review_text_raw: We have worked with Rich for many years and could not be happier to have him in our corner! We know our money is being well taken care of and any recommendations Rich has for us are always based off of our specific situation. 100% recommend.

advisor_name: Richard J. Archer, CDAA, CFA, CFP®, MBA
review_text_raw: I have had the pleasure of working with Rich for almost 20 years. I am always happy with the level of service he and his team provide. It is a great team. Thank you Rich!

advisor_name: Richard J. Archer, CDAA, CFA, CFP®, MBA
review_text_raw: Working with Archer Investments has provided peace of mind in a turbulent world. Rich is an active and thoughtful listener with a wealth of experience and confidence. I’m able to rest easy with him and his team as co-pilots in meeting my financial goals.

My wife Holly and I have been working with Anthony since 2020 and have really appreciated his counsel.  Anthony has given us a strong understanding of our net worth and finances, and he has started us on a clear path for retirement. He has always broken the tasks down into workable steps that we can take together.  I can't say enough about how much better we are, for having the support of Anthony and Cardinal Wealth Management on our journey.  I would highly recommend his services.

I’ve worked with Anthony for several years and appreciate how he has guided me into retirement with sound and reasonable advice regarding my financial security. He is personable, responsive to questions and maintains communication to provide updates and insights beyond annual reviews.

We highly recommend Yulia and Modern Financial Planning.

I have been working with Buddy for many years with help with my father's finances and he has been wonderful.
Providing all the answers it seems when I have questions.

My husband and I have been working with Anthony
Aulffo and the Cardinal Wealth Group since 2019/20. Working with Anthony and Cardinal Wealth has been thoroughly rewarding. I highly recommend Anthony Aulffo and the Cardinal Wealth Group to anyone interested in financial and retirement planning.
Sincerely,
Wynn Dunne

Mike has been invaluable to me to shape my financial situation, ensure the kids college funds, retirement, and other financial goals are met. MFP has been an invaluable resource for me in working toward and planning for my future. I have become accustomed to running any major financial decision past Mike, and I would have it no other way. Really enjoy the newsletter.  Especially how it is simple to understand and doesn't confuse me with a bunch of technical financial terms that I have never heard before.  It breaks things down and makes it easy to understand what they are talking about, but yet it is really educational to me.

David an I go back a way!  I guess we crossed path at the perfect time.  Struggling as a new divorcee/single mother, I did not have the patience for, or time to play with numbers. He guides you, sets up a plan he feels will best fit your needs and helps stay on track with your goals; but he never pressures you in doing something you do not feel comfortable with.  I have learned a lot from David. He set me up with a nice mixed portfolio.  I don't need to "babysit" my investments.  I know I am in good hands;  he always makes himself available.  If you are looking for a knowledgeable, enthusiastic, caring advisor, and yes funny as well (Financial advisors are always so serious) , David is your guy!

My wife and I have been using Anthony for a number of years now. His pleasant and easy-going delivery helps to keep us at ease.
